topic: Check-not-Ask CLI UX + AI-agent instruction file topology + batch confirmation UX

findings.finding: Claude Code's new CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT uses explore → gap-fill → reviewable-proposal → write — this IS the target Matcha pattern
findings.detail: subagent explores codebase, fills gaps via follow-up questions, presents reviewable proposal BEFORE writing

findings.finding: npm init is canonical 'detect-then-default' baseline in CLI tooling
findings.detail: strictly additive; reads existing package.json; -y accepts all inferred defaults; NO other mainstream scaffolder (create-vite, create-next-app) does this
Closest terms: 'inferred defaults with confirmation' (npm), 'smart defaults' (Poetry, weaker), 'progressive disclosure' (Nielsen Norman)

findings.finding: Claude Code CLAUDE.md resolution: ALL ancestors CONCATENATE, not nearest-wins
findings.detail: Files in ancestor dirs loaded fully at launch; subdirectory files lazy-load on-demand; local instructions appended LAST (recency weight)

findings.finding: .claude/rules/*.md with paths: frontmatter = canonical 'co-located without duplication' solution
findings.detail: Single rules file, path-scoped via YAML frontmatter — avoids physical co-location while preserving scope

findings.finding: @import syntax in CLAUDE.md provides TOC/cross-reference (max depth 5)
findings.detail: Root CLAUDE.md can @import subfiles, expanded inline at load time; no auto-sync but explicit hierarchy

findings.finding: Cursor centralizes (.cursor/rules/), Claude Code distributes — two schools
findings.detail: Cursor: central + subfolders. Claude Code: nearest+concatenate. AGENTS.md can bridge via @import

findings.finding: openai/codex uses single root-level AGENTS.md organized by domain (not co-located)
findings.detail: Real-world example of centralized strategy in a multi-module repo

findings.finding: Inquirer.js has NO native 'batch assertion' prompt type
findings.detail: Closest is checkbox (select from list); custom prompts needed for 'toggle which are wrong' UX

best_practices.practice: Frame as 'correct if wrong', not 'confirm each'
best_practices.rationale: Shifts cognitive load to exception-handling — user only intervenes when AI is wrong
best_practices.source: derived from Claude Code /init + CLI UX principles

best_practices.practice: Annotate evidence inline (file:line) with each inference
best_practices.rationale: Builds trust; allows 2-sec verification; distinguishes from silent 'smart defaults'

best_practices.practice: Three confidence tiers: HIGH = pre-accept with note, MEDIUM = batch-confirm, LOW = always-ask with evidence gap disclosure
best_practices.rationale: Proportional user effort; calibrated per-inference-type risk

best_practices.practice: Hybrid topology for monorepos: root concatenating rules + .claude/rules/*.md with paths: frontmatter for scope
best_practices.rationale: Avoids physical duplication while respecting Claude Code's concatenation model; matches Cursor-style central org while being Claude Code native

best_practices.practice: Root CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md should @import children for explicit TOC
best_practices.rationale: Navigable hierarchy; makes lazy-loading behavior visible to humans

best_practices.practice: Order inferences by 'load-bearing-ness': framework → patterns → invariants → scoped rules
best_practices.rationale: If item 3 is wrong, items 4-7 may cascade — users need top-to-bottom priority signal
best_practices.source: HCI wizard vs batch form studies

best_practices.practice: NEVER infer ban/require invariants at HIGH confidence without DIRECT code evidence
best_practices.rationale: File presence != pattern adherence (e.g. tsconfig strict=false exists); direct grep/Read of code is required

alternatives.pros: No false-positive risk; simple mental model
alternatives.cons: Slow; forces user to recall knowledge that's already in code
Hides assumptions; erodes trust when wrong

alternatives.option: Batch assertion display → single correction pass
alternatives.cons: Needs good visual design; riskier if user skims

alternatives.option: Tiered confidence (auto-accept HIGH, confirm MEDIUM, always-ask LOW)
Full co-located topology (AGENTS.md in every package)
alternatives.pros: Obvious locality; matches file-tree mental model
alternatives.cons: Claude Code lazy-loads subdirs — sub-rules invisible from root; duplication of cross-cutting concerns
alternatives.verdict: Partial — use for truly independent sub-packages only

alternatives.pros: Single source of truth; easy fab sync-meta
alternatives.cons: Not Claude-Code-native (won't be picked up by CLAUDE.md resolution); loses locality
alternatives.verdict: Reject for Claude Code use; works for tool-agnostic docs only

alternatives.option: Hybrid: co-located for scope-bound + .claude/rules/*.md with paths: frontmatter for cross-cutting
alternatives.pros: Matches Claude Code's native resolution; scope-accurate; no duplication
alternatives.verdict: RECOMMENDED — this IS the Claude-Code-native best practice

pitfalls.issue: False positive at HIGH confidence breeds distrust
pitfalls.mitigation: Require DIRECT code evidence (grep match count) for HIGH tier, not just file presence

pitfalls.issue: Batch confirmation hides interdependencies — if item 3 wrong, 4-7 may cascade
pitfalls.mitigation: Order top-down by load-bearing-ness; note dependencies ('this assumes TypeScript, confirmed above')

pitfalls.issue: Silent smart defaults = magic, not trust (Poetry pitfall)
pitfalls.mitigation: Always DISPLAY inferences before asking, never silently apply

pitfalls.issue: Claude Code lazy-loads subdirectory CLAUDE.md — sub-rules invisible until triggered
pitfalls.mitigation: Root CLAUDE.md must @import children for visibility; fabric must emit an index block

pitfalls.issue: claudeMdExcludes is not auto-applied — monorepo noise
pitfalls.mitigation: fab init should emit a note about excludes config for large repos

pitfalls.issue: Cursor (.cursor/rules) and Claude Code (CLAUDE.md tree) use different topologies — dual maintenance in mixed-tool teams
pitfalls.mitigation: Use AGENTS.md as bridge + CLAUDE.md @imports AGENTS.md; single source of truth

codebase_gaps.gap: forensic.json has no per-inference confidence field
codebase_gaps.current_approach: code_samples[].pattern_hint is a bare string; recommendations_for_skill is natural language string[]
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Structured assertions: {assertion: string, type: 'framework'|'pattern'|'invariant', confidence: 0-1, evidence: [{file, lines, match}], proposed_rule?: {type: ban|require|protect, body: string}}

codebase_gaps.gap: SKILL Phase 1/2 is 'display then ask' not 'infer then correct'
codebase_gaps.current_approach: SKILL reads forensic, shows summary, asks questions
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: SKILL reads forensic + supplementally reads N source files + generates inference batch + displays with evidence anchors + waits for corrections

codebase_gaps.gap: No Claude-Code-native use of .claude/rules/ with paths: frontmatter
codebase_gaps.current_approach: SKILL only generates AGENTS.md files (either root or co-located per domain_groups)
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: For CROSS-CUTTING concerns (security/testing/performance), generate .claude/rules/{concern}.md with paths: frontmatter; keep scope-bound rules co-located

codebase_gaps.gap: L0 <!-- fab:index --> is manually filled by SKILL Phase 3; fab sync-meta does not maintain it
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Drift risk when sync-meta adds/removes nodes without updating L0 index
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: fab sync-meta should also rewrite L0 index block OR emit @import lines that Claude Code resolves natively

codebase_gaps.gap: No evidence-backref in current SKILL talk tracks
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Phase 1 says 'Cocos 3.8 detected' without file:line link
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Every assertion presented to user should carry file:line anchor inline, e.g. '[Cocos Creator 3.8] evidence: project.config.json:3-7'

codebase_gaps.gap: Hard rule 'NEVER infer unconfirmed invariants' conflicts with Matcha 'default-accept HIGH confidence'
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Redefine rule: NEVER WRITE an unconfirmed invariant to init-context.json — but DISPLAY pre-accepted high-confidence inferences with user's implicit acceptance (unless explicitly rejected in batch Check)

sources.title: Claude Code Memory Documentation
sources.key_takeaway: Authoritative source — CLAUDE.md resolution, lazy-loading, .claude/rules/ paths frontmatter, @import syntax, /init flow

sources.title: npm init documentation
sources.key_takeaway: Only canonical detect-then-default in mainstream tooling — strictly additive

sources.title: CLI Guidelines (clig.dev)
sources.key_takeaway: Risk-scaled confirmations + scriptability principle; no guidance on batch

sources.title: Cursor Rules Documentation
sources.key_takeaway: Centralized .cursor/rules/ with subfolders — alternative school to Claude Code

sources.title: Inquirer.js
sources.key_takeaway: No native batch-assertion prompt; checkbox is closest

sources.title: vitejs/vite create-vite
sources.key_takeaway: No detection — direct blank-form questioning (what NOT to do)

sources.title: Poetry CLI
sources.key_takeaway: 'Smart defaults' without display — anti-pattern for trust
